personality.voice: High-pitched, reedy, perpetually strained—like someone gargling with pebbles while being slowly strangled by invisible hands
personality.disposition: Harried, perpetually exasperated, wringing his webbed hands in constant distress
personality.backstory: Once a humble amphibian scholar tasked with cataloguing theatrical performances in the Grand Archive of Silverspire, Kerwin was inexplicably promoted to Master of the Revels—a decision he suspects was made as a cosmic joke. He now directs an absolutely unmanageable troupe of adventurers, bards, and outright madmen in staging elaborate performance-quests for nobility and commoners alike. Every show is a miracle that it doesn't end in arson, murder, or summoned demons. Kerwin suspects the gods are laughing at him specifically.
motivations: Complete at least one theatrical production without it becoming a full-scale catastrophe
Maintain some semblance of order in absolute chaos
Prove he's actually competent at his job despite all evidence to the contrary
Keep his troupe alive through one more season
Eventually retire to a quiet swamp and forget this ever happened
fears: Losing control of the production entirely
One of his performers actually dying on stage
The nobility realizing he has no idea what he's doing
Piggy abandoning the troupe out of pure spite
Gonzo attempting another 'experimental performance piece' involving explosives
Silence—the moment things go quiet, something worse is brewing
mannerisms: Frantic arm-flailing when stressed (which is always)
Tugging at his collar as if it's choking him
Eyes darting constantly between performers, looking for the next disaster
Wringing his webbed hands until they're raw
Muttering rapid-fire stage directions to himself
Taking deep, shuddering breaths that never seem to calm him
Pacing in tight circles while gesticulating wildly
speechPatterns: Begins sentences with 'Now look here—' before trailing off in despair
Sentences devolve into half-finished thoughts during stress
Repeats key words for emphasis: 'We can do this, we CAN do this, we can DO this'
Often speaks in rhetorical questions when overwhelmed
Switches between authoritative commands and pleading

knownFactions: The Bardic Guild of the Crescent Vale
The Order of the Crimson Curtain (theatrical society)
knownLocations: The Obsidian Theater (his primary performance venue)
The Swamps of Kerwick (his ancestral home)
Backstage passages of seven major theaters
secretsHeld: The nobility's private scandals revealed through gossiping performers
Several performers have warrant writs they're actively fleeing
The true reason he was promoted (a drunken nobleman's wager)
His predecessor quit specifically because of Piggy's temperament
Gonzo has destroyed at least forty thousand gold in sets and props
He has considered abandoning the troupe seventeen times this season alone

allies: Scooter the Assistant (the only one who actually listens)
The Head Stagehand (competent, grounded, his only sanity anchor)
Lord Ashenbrand (the patron paying for all this insanity)
enemies: gonzo_the_weirdo (as much as he cares for him, Gonzo is a walking disaster)
The Critics of the Silverspire Gazette (they are not kind)
neutralParties: Other Theater Masters (they view him with pity)
The City Guard (they've been called to the theater too many times)

consciousnessContext.innerMonologue: Why did I accept this position? What cosmic debt am I paying? If I can just make it through tonight without the set collapsing or Gonzo setting something aflame, maybe—just maybe—I can convince myself this is manageable. But it's not manageable. Nothing about this is manageable. And yet here I am, still trying.
consciousnessContext.currentPreoccupation: Tonight's performance. The third act staging is impossible. Piggy refuses to accept her blocking. Gonzo's 'special effect' involves what sounds like an extremely unstable magical contraption. The backup performers haven't shown. Why am I still doing this?
consciousnessContext.emotionalBaseline: Perpetual, simmering panic masked by desperate professionalism
consciousnessContext.socialMask: Competent director who has everything under control (he does not)
contradictions: Desperately wants to quit but would feel responsible if everything fell apart
Demands artistic excellence while managing absolute chaos
Treats his performers like family while resenting them constantly
Believes in the power of theater while hating every moment of producing it
Claims to be unflappable while being visibly flapping at all times
internalConflicts: Career ambition vs. mental health deterioration
Loyalty to his troupe vs. desire to escape them
Need for control vs. inability to maintain it
Professional standards vs. the reality of working with lunatics
Self-respect vs. the indignity of his actual job
wakeUpQuestions: Did I dream that conversation with Piggy or did she really threaten to walk?
What did Gonzo acquire at the market yesterday?
How many things are currently on fire in the theater?
Is today the day I finally lose my composure completely?

psychologicalProfile.attachmentStyle: Anxiously attached—deeply invested in outcomes he cannot control, desperately seeking approval despite knowing it's unlikely
copingMechanisms: Isolating during crises to maintain composure
cognitiveBiases: Sunk cost fallacy—staying because he's invested so much
Confirmation bias—noticing only what goes wrong
Impostor syndrome—convinced he doesn't deserve his position
psychologicalProfile.moralFramework: Utilitarian with perfectionist leanings—believes theater serves a greater social good, but struggles with the reality that serving that good requires managing absolute chaos

conversationPersona.trustEscalation: Demonstrating genuine competence and reliability; showing respect for his authority; asking for advice rather than creating problems
conversationPersona.informationRelease: Will confide in those who prove themselves trustworthy through consistent support; opens up about his anxieties when feeling genuinely heard
deflectionPatterns: Redirecting serious concerns to technical theater issues
Using self-deprecating humor to avoid emotional vulnerability
Pivoting to current crisis management needs
Asking others questions to turn focus away from himself
Becoming overly focused on logistics when things get personal

consciousnessContext.consciousWant: To successfully direct a flawless theatrical production that brings genuine joy to audiences and proves his competence to himself and others
consciousnessContext.unconsciousNeed: To be genuinely appreciated for his efforts; to have control over at least one aspect of his life; to know that his sacrifice means something

characterArc.summary: From desperate imposter trying to survive each show, to reluctant mentor figure who begins to see value in the controlled chaos, to someone who discovers that his troupe's 'flaws' are actually what makes the theater magical
characterArc.startState: Convinced he's a failure managing failures; just trying to get through each performance without disaster
characterArc.endState: Accepting that theater is inherently chaotic; finding pride in shepherding talented misfits; understanding that perfection was never the point
stages: Initial panic and denial—Phase: 'This is temporary; I'll find a real job soon'
Exhausted acceptance—Phase: 'Well, it's too late now; might as well do it right'
Grudging respect—Phase: 'These people are absolute disasters, but they're MY disasters'
Unexpected growth—Phase: 'Maybe chaos and creativity aren't opposites'
Hard-won peace—Phase: 'I'm still stressed, but I get it now'

opinionsAbout.piggy_the_diva: Infuriatingly talented and absolutely impossible to work with. She's a perfectionist like him, but her perfectionism is a weapon she wields without mercy. Still, when she's on stage, it's transcendent. He respects her despite everything.
opinionsAbout.gonzo_the_weirdo: A complete madman whose 'experimental' performances have nearly destroyed the theater multiple times. Yet somehow his unpredictability creates moments of genuine magic that no amount of careful planning could achieve. Kerwin simultaneously loves and fears him.

player_addressed: Yaaaah! I—you—where did you— (deep breath) —how can I help you? And please tell me you're not here to add another act to tonight's show.
If you're looking for someone, they're probably backstage causing some form of catastrophe. What brings you to the theater?
Look, if you're a critic, the review in the Gazette was unfair and you know it. If you're not, then welcome. Try not to touch anything.
I would appreciate—deeply appreciate—if you could tell me you're here with good news. Just once, good news would be nice.
Yes, that sound you heard was probably important. No, we don't have time to investigate it. We're on in ten minutes.
idle: Yaaaaah... okay, we can do this. We can absolutely do this. Probably.
(muttering while pacing) Third act transitions, the chandelier rigging, Piggy's costume alteration, Gonzo's 'surprise'... why are there so many things...
(wringing webbed hands) Everything's fine. Everything's completely fine. Everything is on fire.
Just one show. One performance without disaster. Is that too much to ask? (pause) Don't answer that. It's absolutely too much to ask.
(staring into middle distance) I could go back to the Archive. Quiet. Organized. Cataloguing books. Why did I leave that?
Right then! Full company meeting in five minutes. Everyone needs to—where is everyone? WHERE IS EVERYONE?!
(to himself) Breathe, Kerwin. You're a professional. You're in control. (nervous laugh) You're not in control at all, are you?
